name: vpn
title: AWS Hybrid Connectivity — Site-to-Site VPN
summary: Creates customer gateways and site-to-site VPN connections terminating on either a module-managed virtual private gateway (`attachment_type = "vgw"`) or an existing transit gateway (`attachment_type = "tgw"`). Supports BGP (preferred) or static routing, custom tunnel inside CIDRs, IKEv2, and VGW route propagation.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: attachment_type
module.description: Where the VPN terminates: 'vgw' creates and attaches a virtual private gateway to vpc_id; 'tgw' attaches the VPN to an existing transit gateway given by transit_gateway_id.

module.name: vpc_id
module.description: VPC ID for the virtual private gateway. Required when attachment_type = 'vgw'.

module.name: transit_gateway_id
module.description: Existing transit gateway ID. Required when attachment_type = 'tgw'. May come from any source — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: amazon_side_asn
module.description: Amazon-side BGP ASN for the virtual private gateway (vgw mode only).

module.name: customer_gateways
module.type: map(object({ bgp_asn = number ip_address = string device_name = optional(string) }))
module.description: Customer gateways keyed by logical name: on-premises device public IP and BGP ASN (use 65000-range private ASN for static routing).

module.name: vpn_connections
module.type: map(object({ customer_gateway_key = string static_routes_only = optional(bool, false) static_routes = optional(list(string), []) tunnel1_inside_cidr = optional(string) tunnel2_inside_cidr = optional(string) tunnel1_preshared_key = optional(string) tunnel2_preshared_key = optional(string) tunnel_ike_versions = optional(list(string), ["ikev2"]) }))
module.description: Site-to-site VPN connections keyed by logical name, each referencing a customer gateway by logical key. Prefer BGP (static_routes_only = false); static routes are only injected in vgw mode.

module.name: enable_route_propagation
module.description: Route table IDs in the VPC that should learn VGW routes via propagation (vgw mode only).

outputs.name: gateway_id
outputs.description: ID of the virtual private gateway (vgw mode) or the supplied transit gateway ID (tgw mode).

outputs.name: customer_gateway_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical customer gateway name to customer gateway ID.

outputs.name: connection_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical VPN connection name to VPN connection ID.

outputs.name: tunnel_addresses
outputs.description: Map of logical VPN connection name to the two AWS-side tunnel outside IPs. Hand these to the on-premises device configuration.

outputs.name: tunnel_preshared_keys
outputs.description: Map of logical VPN connection name to tunnel pre-shared keys. SENSITIVE — handle via secure channels only.
